seo.title: 100 Angular resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Angular resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides enterprise Angular developers with the specific tools, patterns, and commands required to modernize large-scale applications. It focuses on the transition from module-based architectures to standalone components, the implementation of signals for state management, and the optimization of bundle sizes for high-performance corporate environments.

items.title: Standalone Migration Schematic
items.description: Run 'ng generate @angular/core:standalone' to automatically convert modules, components, and pipes to the standalone format in existing codebases.

items.title: toSignal Interop
items.description: Use 'toSignal(observable$)' from @angular/core/rxjs-interop to bridge legacy RxJS streams into the new reactive signal system for template binding.

items.title: Functional Interceptors
items.description: Replace class-based interceptors with 'HttpInterceptorFn' and register them using 'provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([...]))' in the application config.

items.title: inject() Function
items.description: Utilize 'inject(Token)' for dependency injection in component constructors and field initializers to simplify inheritance and improve type safety.

items.title: Computed Signals
items.description: Implement 'computed(() => logic)' to create derived state that only re-evaluates when its dependencies change, reducing unnecessary change detection cycles.

items.title: Effect() for Side Effects
items.description: Use 'effect()' within an injection context to perform logging, manual DOM manipulation, or synchronization with local storage based on signal changes.

items.title: provideRouter Configuration
items.description: Migrate from AppRoutingModule to functional routing using 'provideRouter(routes, withComponentInputBinding())' to enable direct signal input mapping from URL params.

items.title: Signal-based Inputs
items.description: Replace @Input() with 'input()' and 'input.required()' to gain native signal reactivity and remove the need for ngOnChanges in enterprise components.

items.title: Model Inputs
items.description: Use 'model()' inputs to create writable signals that support two-way data binding with parent components without boilerplate @Output emitters.

items.title: EnvironmentInjector Usage
items.description: Use EnvironmentInjector.runInContext() to execute logic within the application's DI context when working outside of standard component lifecycles.

items.title: Deferrable Views (@defer)
items.description: Implement '@defer (on viewport)' blocks to automatically lazy-load heavy components like charts or maps only when they enter the user's view.

items.title: NgOptimizedImage Directive
items.description: Replace tags with the 'NgOptimizedImage' directive to automate lazy loading, priority hints, and aspect ratio enforcement for better LCP scores.

items.title: Nx Computation Caching
items.description: Configure Nx Cloud or local caching in 'nx.json' to avoid re-building and re-testing unchanged libraries in large monorepo structures.

items.title: Angular Budgets
items.description: Define 'budgets' in 'angular.json' to trigger build errors when bundle sizes exceed specific thresholds (e.g., 500kb for initial load).

items.title: Source Map Explorer
items.description: Run 'source-map-explorer dist/main.js' to identify which third-party libraries are contributing most to the final bundle size.

items.title: Zoneless Change Detection
items.description: Experiment with 'provideExperimentalZonelessChangeDetection()' to remove zone.js dependency and improve performance in signal-heavy applications.

items.title: Component Level Code Splitting
items.description: Use dynamic imports with 'loadComponent' in the router config to split large enterprise features into separate chunks.

items.title: Esbuild Builder
items.description: Update 'angular.json' to use '@angular-devkit/build-angular:browser-esbuild' for significantly faster build times compared to the legacy Webpack builder.

items.title: OnPush Strategy
items.description: Enforce 'ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush' globally to limit change detection only to components whose inputs have changed or signals have emitted.

items.title: Preconnect Hints
items.description: Add '<link rel="preconnect">' in index.html for API domains and CDN assets to reduce DNS lookup latency in enterprise networks.

items.title: NgRx SignalStore
items.description: Implement '@ngrx/signals' for a lightweight, functional state management approach that integrates directly with Angular's signal primitives.

items.title: Component Harnesses
items.description: Use 'TestbedHarnessEnvironment' with Angular Material harnesses to write tests that are resilient to internal DOM changes.

items.title: Spectator for Unit Tests
items.description: Adopt 'ngneat/spectator' to reduce boilerplate when setting up the TestBed and mocking dependencies like services and components.

items.title: Cypress Component Testing
items.description: Configure 'cypress/angular' to run isolated component tests in a real browser environment, bypassing the limitations of JSDOM.

items.title: NgMocks for DI
items.description: Use 'MockBuilder' and 'MockProvider' from 'ng-mocks' to isolate the unit under test by automatically stubbing all external dependencies.

items.title: takeUntilDestroyed Operator
items.description: Apply 'takeUntilDestroyed()' from @angular/core/rxjs-interop to automatically unsubscribe from RxJS streams when the component is destroyed.

items.title: NgRx Entity
items.description: Utilize '@ngrx/entity' to manage normalized collections of data, providing O(1) lookup times for large datasets in enterprise dashboards.

items.title: Storybook Integration
items.description: Set up Storybook with 'npx storybook@latest init' to document and develop UI components in isolation from the main enterprise application logic.

items.title: Playwright for E2E
items.description: Implement Playwright for end-to-end testing to leverage its superior multi-tab support and faster execution speeds compared to older tools.

items.title: HttpTestingController
items.description: Use 'HttpTestingController' to mock backend responses and verify that specific API calls were made with the correct headers and payloads.

pro_tips: Always use 'track' in the new @for loop syntax to prevent full DOM re-renders of lists when data updates.
Enable 'strict' mode in tsconfig.json to catch null pointer exceptions and type mismatches during build time.
Prefer functional 'guards' over class-based 'CanActivate' to reduce the complexity of the dependency injection tree.
Utilize 'provideZoneChangeDetection({ eventCoalescing: true })' to group multiple events into a single change detection cycle.
Use the Angular DevTools Chrome extension specifically to profile 'Change Detection' to find components triggering unnecessary updates.
